title: mercurialis corsica
intro: Mercurialis corsica, also known as Corsican wood-barrenwort or Corsican mercury, is a perennial plant species native to the island of Corsica, belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Mercurialis corsica include reducing inflammation, improving digestive health, and acting as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often used to make infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
